commit.message:
fix(settings): respect $XDG_CONFIG_HOME for user config path

SettingsLoader was hardcoding ~/.config/color-scheme/settings.toml as
the default user config path, silently ignoring $XDG_CONFIG_HOME.

On systems where $XDG_CONFIG_HOME is set to a non-default location,
user settings were never loaded because the loader looked in the wrong
place.

Changes:
- loader.py: read $XDG_CONFIG_HOME via os.environ.get, fall back to
  ~/.config only when the variable is unset; remove stale Args docstring
  block (behaviour is covered by tests)
- test_loader.py: add three unit tests covering XDG path, ~/.config
  fallback, and explicit path precedence (BHV-0037)
- run-smoke-tests.sh: add test_xdg_config_home() end-to-end suite
  (XDG dir loaded, fallback succeeds, missing config silently skipped)
- docs: update settings-api.md, configure-settings.md, and
  architecture.md to reflect XDG-aware path; register BHV-0037 in
  both verification tables

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

state

Add post-push PR review polling instruction for agents +semver: feature (#420)

## Business Value

Agents that push fixes to open PRs currently have no authoritative
instruction governing what to do next. Without guidance, an agent may
poll for reviewer feedback too early (before reviewers have had time to
read the push), batch multiple comment fixes into a single commit
(breaking traceability), substitute a top-level PR comment when it
cannot resolve a thread (hiding unfinished work), or loop without bound
if reviewers keep adding feedback. This instruction closes all those
gaps with clear, RFC 2119-strength rules that every agent can follow
deterministically.

## Common Use Cases

- **CoV PR Review + Remediation agent** finishes addressing review
comments, pushes to the branch, and needs to know: sleep how long? poll
how? reply and resolve in what order? what if `gh` is the only tool
available?
- **Any agent** merging a hotfix to an open PR follows the same loop
without needing custom logic.
- **Automated CI bots** that summarize or label PRs can use the polling
rules to know when to back off and wait for human review.

## How It Works

```
[TRIGGER: code pushed to a branch with an open PR]
IF no open PR THEN EXIT
LOOP (max 20 iterations)
  SLEEP 300 seconds
  POLL for unresolved review comments/threads (MCP → gh CLI → stop+report)
  IF no new unaddressed comments THEN EXIT LOOP
  FOR EACH comment (one at a time)
    fix → commit → push → reply → resolve (or decline+leave-open)
  END FOR
END LOOP
```

Key design decisions:
- **300-second sleep** before first poll — gives reviewers time to
inspect the push before the agent reacts.
- **One comment = one commit** — keeps fix-to-feedback mapping
auditable.
- **MCP preferred, `gh` CLI fallback** — specific `gh api graphql`
commands provided for thread replies and resolution so there is no
ambiguity about which endpoint to call.
- **`isOutdated` threads skipped** — GitHub does not reliably allow
resolving outdated threads; the agent logs them and moves on rather than
hitting an API error loop.
- **Max 20 iteration cap** — prevents runaway polling in adversarial
scenarios.
- **Stop-and-report on tool failure** — agents must not substitute a
top-level PR comment for a failed per-thread action.

## Files Changed

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `.github/instructions/pr-review-polling.instructions.md` | **New** —
canonical instruction (applyTo: `**`) |
| `.cursor/rules/pr-review-polling.mdc` | **New** — Cursor rule mirror
(alwaysApply: true) |
| `.github/agents/CoV-mississippi-pr-review-mcp.agent.md` | Updated
section 7 to reference the canonical instruction instead of duplicating
its content |
| `.github/instructions/pull-request-reviews.instructions.md` | Added
reference link to the new polling instruction |
| `.cursor/rules/pull-request-reviews.mdc` | Mirror updated with
matching reference link + timestamp |

## Quality Gates

- [x] Zero build/analyzer warnings (no `.cs` files changed)
- [x] `markdownlint-cli2` — 0 errors on all 5 changed files
- [x] MDC mirror is semantically identical to canonical instruction
- [x] CoV agent section 7 no longer duplicates canonical rules (drift
risk eliminated)

## Migration Notes

No breaking changes. The new instruction is additive. The CoV agent's
section 7 now references the canonical instruction rather than
duplicating it — agents reading the CoV agent file will be directed to
the authoritative source.

## Related Issues

Companion to the CoV Mississippi PR Review + Remediation agent
(`CoV-mississippi-pr-review-mcp.agent.md`).

commit.message:
v0.1.68: Fix worldgen origin GPS and enumerate_surface_chunks

Two bugs caused worldgen to generate 18K empty chunks:

1. Wrong origin GPS: worldgen used GPS(-27.3996, 153.1871, 2.0m) instead
   of the client's Kangaroo Point origin. terrain.rs places voxels at
   origin_voxel.y + (srtm_ell - origin_gps.alt), so mismatched origin
   causes terrain to be placed thousands of voxels outside chunk bounds.
   Fix: query SRTM+EGM96 at base_lat=-27.4672, base_lon=153.03 (same
   as metaworld_alpha.rs) to get correct ellipsoidal altitude.

2. Wrong enumerate_surface_chunks: used absolute ECEF_Y of region centre
   to find surface chunk Y. At Brisbane (lon≈153°), lat/lon changes the
   ECEF_Y component by ~440 voxels per 0.005° even at constant altitude
   (spherical geometry). Region centre was 2 chunks above the actual
   terrain layer, so worldgen generated chunks 44844/45 while terrain.rs
   placed voxels in chunks 44841/42 — no overlap.
   Fix: use origin_chunk_y - 1 as surface_chunk_y (consistent with how
   terrain.rs computes surface_voxel_y = origin_voxel.y + delta).

Also: default extra_y_layers 1→2 to cover: Y-1 (river/sea level terrain),
Y (clifftop/surface), Y+1 (building tops).

Result: 9108 Y=44841 chunks with real terrain (up to 174KB each),
3720 Y=44842 chunks with surface features. 27324 chunks in 9.6s.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(auth): resolve session loss from SameSite cookie mismatch (BUG-04)

Root cause: OAuth2Controller set cookies with SameSite=Lax, but AuthController
used SameSite=Strict. After token rotation, browsers could maintain duplicate
cookies (stale Lax + new Strict), and getFirst() always read the stale one,
causing 'revoked token reuse' failures.

Fix Bazel test suite: all UI tests pass, server tests pass, CI uses ubuntu-latest (#6182)

`bazelisk test //...` was broken due to a combination of Bazel build
config errors, `@/` alias vs relative-path module-instance mismatches in
vitest mocks, and JSDOM 27 incompatibilities.

## CI / Bazel Config
- All GitHub Actions workflows (`ci.yml`, `bot-daily.yml`,
`bot-hourly.yml`, `release.yml`): replaced `oci-runner` with
`ubuntu-latest`
- `.bazelrc`: replaced invalid `;` inline comment with `#`
- `MODULE.bazel`: added `in_yaml_go_yaml_v3`,
`org_golang_google_genproto_googleapis_api`,
`org_golang_google_genproto_googleapis_rpc` to `use_repo`
- `server/go.mod`: added `go.yaml.in/yaml/v3 v3.0.4 // indirect`
- Proto `BUILD.bazel` files: fixed compiler list and `@googleapis`
annotation deps
- `ui/BUILD.bazel`: removed `ESLINT_USE_FLAT_CONFIG: false` to enable
flat ESLint config
- `ui/eslint.config.mjs`: fixed plugin references that weren't loadable
in the Bazel sandbox

## Vitest mock path fixes (module-instance mismatch)
In the Bazel sandbox, `vi.mock('../../lib/client')` and
`vi.mock('@/lib/client')` resolve to different module instances. All
mocks must use the same path the component imports. Fixed in
`analytics-dashboard.test.tsx` and `secrets-manager.test.tsx`:

```ts
// Before — mock doesn't intercept component imports
vi.mock('../../lib/client', () => ({ apiClient: { ... } }));

// After — matches what the component imports
vi.mock('@/lib/client', () => ({ apiClient: { ... } }));
```

`navigation.test.tsx` similarly mocked `@/components/ui/sidebar`,
`@/components/user-context`, and `@/contexts/keyboard-shortcuts-context`
via alias paths to avoid context-instance mismatches across
`SidebarProvider` / `useUser` / `useSidebar`.

## JSDOM 27 / fake-timer fixes
- `global-search.tsx`: extracted `window.location.reload()` into a named
`reloadPage` export; `global-search.test.tsx` switched to `vi.hoisted()`
and tests observable behavior (dialog close) instead of mocking a
non-configurable browser property
- `dashboard-grid.test.tsx`: switched to `vi.useFakeTimers({
shouldAdvanceTime: true })` so `waitFor` polling works with fake timers;
mocked `fetch` so `localStorage.setItem` is reached inside the debounce
`try` block; advanced timers by 1100 ms to exceed the component's 1000
ms debounce

## Type declarations
Added `ui/src/types/react-syntax-highlighter.d.ts` to declare the ESM
sub-path imports (`/dist/esm/prism-light`,
`/dist/esm/languages/prism/*`, `/dist/esm/styles/prism`,
`/dist/esm/light`, `/dist/esm/languages/hljs/*`) used across multiple
components, resolving `TS7016` errors.

commit.message:
Fix space key not working with Kitty keyboard protocol

When VSCode's Kitty keyboard protocol is enabled, space is sent as
escape sequence \x1b[32;1u. Textual's XTermParser creates Key('space', None)
with character=None (since the sequence length > 1), which makes
is_printable=False and TextArea silently drops the keypress.

Fix by detecting this case in InputPane._on_key and restoring the
character attribute before delegating to TextArea.

commit.message:
Fix PerfView CPU merge failure: remove /ThreadTime, add /StackCompression:false

Remove /ThreadTime from PerfView CPU collector to avoid generating massive
context-switch traces (1.3GB ETL) that cause ETW merge failure 0x1069.

Fix tvOS device communication failures causing false TIMED_OUT and APP_CRASH after successful runs (#1551)

* Initial plan

* Fix tvOS device communication failures causing false TIMED_OUT and APP_CRASH results

Three layered fixes:

1. DeviceLogCapturer: Add 2-minute timeouts to 'log collect' and 'log show' processes
   in StopCapture() to prevent indefinite blocking when Apple TV device log streaming
   is broken (fixes apple run TIMED_OUT after mlaunch exits with expected code).

2. ResultFileHandler: Add retry with backoff (up to 3 attempts, 5s/10s/20s delays)
   to devicectl file copy in CopyResultsAsync() to handle transient device communication
   errors (com.apple.Mercury.error 1000, RSD error 0xE8000003).

3. TestReporter.ParseResult(): When Success=true was already set by CollectDeviceResult()
   (test run completed via app end signal), but the results file is missing (devicectl
   failed), return Succeeded instead of Crashed. Device communication failure after
   successful test completion is an infrastructure issue, not an app crash.

4. AppTester: Skip crash report retrieval when test run already completed and file copy
   fails, replacing misleading 'app may have crashed' message with accurate warning.

Adds unit tests for retry behavior and the new TestReporter.ParseResult() path.

feat: context-aware completion provider for .strm files (#27)

## Summary

- Context-aware completions scoped to what is grammatically valid at the
cursor position
- `in`/`out` snippet variants auto-pop on space after keyword (no
Ctrl+Space needed)
- After `in source -> queue` or `out queue -> dest`, space triggers
options scoped by source/dest type (CSV/S3/DB)
- `node name(inputs)(outputs)` space trigger offers all node-level
options (`lang=`, `exec_when=`, `where=`, `getfact=`, `setfact=`,
`getsig=`, `setsig=`, `getdyn=`, `setdyn=`)
- Model continuation (`model` line or indented line below it): suggests
`type=`, `label=`, `exceptq=`, `scenario=`, `unittest=`, `modelname=`;
excludes options already defined on the same line or preceding
continuation lines
- Top-level keywords cleaned up: only proper statements (`name`, `type`,
`label`, `descr`, `in`, `out`, `node`, `model`, `subprocess`); removed
ANTLR token dump and line-option keywords (`lang`, `db`, `mode`)
- Fixed `model` snippet: grammar has no node name — `model
(input1)(output1)` not `model nodeName(...)`
- `createCompletionProvider(csvCache)` extracted as exported factory for
unit testing
- 72 unit tests added covering all contexts and scoping rules

## Test plan

### Top-level (empty line)
- [x] Ctrl+Space on empty line shows: `name`, `type`, `label`, `descr`,
`in`, `out`, `node`, `model`, `subprocess`
- [x] Does NOT show: `lang`, `db`, `mode`, `exceptq`, or ANTLR document
tokens (node/queue names)

### `in` snippets
- [x] Type `in ` (space) → `in (CSV)`, `in (DB)`, `in (S3)` auto-pop
without Ctrl+Space
- [x] `in (CSV)` snippet inserts `file.csv -> input_queue` (no `in `
prefix duplication)

### `in` line options (after `->`)
- [x] `in input.csv -> raw_input ` (space) → shows `where=`, `name=` —
NOT `db=`, NOT `compress`
- [x] `in public.transactions -> db_input ` → shows `db=`, `where=`,
`name=`
- [x] `in s3://bucket/path -> s3_input ` → shows nothing (S3 inputs have
no options)
- [x] Already-used options are excluded (e.g. `where=` already on line →
not re-suggested)

### `out` snippets
- [x] Type `out ` (space) → `out (CSV)`, `out (DB)`, `out (S3)` auto-pop
without Ctrl+Space
- [x] `out (CSV)` snippet inserts `output_queue -> output.csv` (no `out
` prefix duplication)

### `out` line options (after `->`)
- [x] `out queue -> output.csv ` → shows `compress`, `exec_when=` — NOT
`db=`, NOT `mode=`
- [x] `out queue -> s3://bucket/path ` → shows `mode=`, `compress`,
`exec_when=` — NOT `db=`
- [x] `out queue -> analytics.results ` → shows `db=`, `compress`,
`exec_when=` — NOT `mode=`
- [x] Already-used flags excluded (e.g. `compress` already on line → not
re-suggested)

### `node` line options
- [x] `node filternode(raw_input)(filtered) ` (space after closing
paren) → auto-pops `lang=`, `exec_when=`, `where=`, `getfact=`,
`setfact=`, `getsig=`, `setsig=`, `getdyn=`, `setdyn=`
- [x] Options already on the line are excluded (e.g. `lang=go ` →
`lang=` not re-suggested)

### `model` snippet and continuation
- [x] Selecting `model` snippet inserts `model (input1)(output1)` — no
node name
- [x] After `model (enriched)(scored)`, Enter + indent, space or
Ctrl+Space shows: `type=`, `label=`, `exceptq=`, `scenario=`,
`unittest=`, `modelname=`
- [x] Options defined on previous continuation lines are excluded
- [x] Options defined on the model line itself (same line as cursor) are
excluded
- [x] When all 6 options are defined, suggestion list is empty

### No duplicate trigger
- [x] Accept `in (CSV)` snippet, tab through, press space after queue
name → shows `where=`/`name=` only (not `in (CSV)` snippets again)
- [x] Same for `out` — no re-trigger of out snippets after space on a
completed line

feat(vscode): wire InlineDecorations to SSE eval output

Add OnEvalResult and OnEvalStarted callbacks to LiveTestingCallbacks and
wire them to InlineDecorations in the SSE event dispatcher.

SSE event flow:
  eval_started -> markDecorationsStale (stale ghost text appears immediately)
  eval_result  -> showInlineResult with server-computed durationMs

The SSE path complements the existing HTTP-response path in evalSelection():
evals triggered by other clients (Neovim, VS, another VS Code window) now
also produce inline ghost text in open editors that match filePath.

Event schema used:
  eval_started: { filePath, blockStartLine }
  eval_result:  { filePath, blockStartLine, output, success, durationMs }

blockStartLine is 1-based (server convention); converted to 0-based for
VS Code TextEditor line numbers.

feat(daemon): route error paths to structured OTel logging

- Add durationMs field to eval_result SSE event so timing is observable

  mark decorations stale without polling
- Document start-sagefs-otel.bat: add Jaeger quick-start instructions

The Log module already routes through ILogger -> OTel when the daemon
is running; the file sink (logging.AddFile) already captures all
structured events. This commit completes the SSE telemetry surface so
eval timing flows end-to-end from daemon to editor decorations.

fix(sanitize): use errors.As with pgconn.PgError instead of string matching (#62)

IsDuplicateKey and IsDatabaseError previously used strings.Contains on
the error message, which is fragile — a table name or message containing
the SQLSTATE code could produce a false positive.

Use errors.As(*pgconn.PgError) with direct Code comparison for precise,
type-safe detection. pgconn is already a transitive dependency via pgx/v5.

Update tests and fuzz test to construct *pgconn.PgError values; the fuzz
test now verifies that plain errors.New values never match, and that the
Code field is the sole discriminator.
